title: phegopteris tomentosa
intro: Phegopteris tomentosa, also known as the woolly polypody fern, is a species of fern that is native to East Asia and is often found in shaded, humid environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phegopteris tomentosa include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote digestive health and reduce stress.
